NEGLIGENCE CAUSE OF ACTION IS BARRED BY STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS AND PLAINTIFF FAILED TO ALLEGE FACTS RE: DUTY OF CARE AS LENDER. SEE NYMARK V. HEART FED. SAV. & LOAN (1991) 231 CAL. APP.3D 1089; DAS V. BANK OF AMERICA (2010) 186 CAL.APP.4TH 727. FRAUD CAUSES OF ACTION ARE NOT PLEAD WITH REQUISITE PARTICULARITY AS TO EACH DEFENDANT SEPARATE, INSTEAD ALLEGATIONS RE: MISREPRESENTATION (WHO, WHEN, IN WHAT MANNER) ARE MADE AS TO "CITI DEFENDANTS." PLAINTIFF FAILS TO PROVIDE ANY MEANINGFUL OPPOSITION REGARDING UNAVAILABILITY OF FRAUD CAUSES OF ACTION FOR REPRESENTATIONS OF FUTURE EVENTS. THE PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B) ( SEE OPPOSITION 4: 11-25). JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER, JR.; CLERK: R. GOULDING; NOT REPORTED

DEMURRER TO 4TH AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-05-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Elizabeth Karnazes, Plaintiff in Pro Per 650-345-9200

The Court hears argument by plaintiff Elizabeth Kqarnazes.
Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument,adopt the tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT CITIMORTGAGE, INC. ERRONEOUSLY SUED AS, CITIBANK, N.A.'s DEMURRER TO 4TH AMENDED COMPLAINT is Sustained without leave to amend. Negligence cause of action is barred by statute of limitations and Plaintiff failed to allege facts re: duty of care as lender. See Nymark v. Heart Fed. Sav. & Loan (1991) 231 Cal. App.3d 1089; Das v. Bank of America (2010) 186 Cal.App.4th 727. Fraud causes of action are not plead with requisite particularity as to each defendant separate, instead allegations re: misrepresentation (who, when, in what manner) are made as to "Citi Defendants." Plaintiff fails to provide any meaningful opposition regarding unavailability of fraud causes of action for representations of future events. (See Opposition 4: 11-25). The prevailing party to submit a proposed order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b) Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: R. Goulding; Not Reported

Off Calendar Mar-07-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Stephanie A. Chambers, Esq. of Locke Lord LLP, (415-318-8818), for CitiMortgage, Defendant and moving partY.
Elizabeth Karnazes, Plaintiff in pro per and opposing party, (650-345-9200), APPEARING THROUGH COURT CALL.

ARGUED. Court adopts the tentative ruling with modification as follows: DEFENDANT CITIMORTGAGE, INC.'s DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained with 90-day leave to amend to allege facts to support each element of her causes of action for intentional tort, negligence, breach of contract and fraud. Defendant's demurer was properly calendared in Department 501 (Real Property/Housing Court) as Plaintiff's Second Amended Complaint filed on December 30, 2013 indicates that it is a complaint for "other (specify) fraud, unlawful foreclosure, etc." Court further rules that there will be no more continuances to be granted to amend the complaint, and Court will no longer grant leave to amend complaint if third amended complaint will not allege facts to support each element of her causes of action for intentional tort, negligence, breach of contract and fraud. Moving party and prevailing party is ordered to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge Lilian Sing, Clerk: M. Olopernes, Not reported (D501)
